levels.title: Absolute Beginner
levels.milestone: Read German aloud with correct sounds, and greet, introduce yourself and count.
canDo: Pronounce the sounds English does not have: ä, ö, ü, the ch of ich and the ch of Buch, initial z, w and v, and the r in the throat.
Use guten Tag / danke / bitte / tschüss naturally, and tell du from Sie.
Say your name, where you are from and where you live with 'Ich heiße...' and 'Ich komme aus...'; count to 20.
Write German the way German is written: every noun with a capital letter, ß after a long vowel and ss after a short one.

levels.title: Beginner (Anfänger)
levels.milestone: Handle simple daily exchanges using the present tense, the nominative and accusative, and verb-second word order.
canDo: Conjugate sein, haben and regular verbs in the present, plus the vowel-changing verbs (fahren, sprechen, sehen, essen).
Use der/die/das and ein/eine with the nominative and accusative, and learn every new noun with its article.
Put the verb in second position in statements and first in yes/no questions, and ask with wer, was, wo, wann, wie and warum.
Use the modal verbs können, müssen, wollen and möchten with the second verb at the end of the sentence.
Talk about the past with the perfect of common verbs, choosing haben or sein correctly.

descriptors: I can recognise familiar words and very basic phrases concerning myself, my family and immediate concrete surroundings when people speak slowly and clearly.
I can understand familiar names, words and very simple sentences, for example on notices and posters or in catalogues.
I can interact in a simple way provided the other person is prepared to repeat or rephrase things at a slower rate of speech and help me formulate what I'm trying to say.
I can ask and answer simple questions in areas of immediate need or on very familiar topics.
I can use simple phrases and sentences to describe where I live and people I know.
I can write a short, simple postcard, for example sending holiday greetings.
I can fill in forms with personal details, for example entering my name, nationality and address on a hotel registration form.

exam.name: Goethe-Zertifikat A1: Start Deutsch 1
exam.description: The first rung of the Goethe ladder: asking for and giving simple information about everyday topics, introducing yourself and answering basic questions about your person. This checkpoint practises its four-part format.
sections: Sprechen, speaking, conducted in a group with topic cards
exam.passRule: Die vier Teile werden zusammen bewertet. Bestanden ab 60% insgesamt.

levels.title: Elementary (Grundstufe)
levels.milestone: Cope with routine matters at the Amt, at the doctor and at work, using the dative, the past tenses and subordinate clauses.
canDo: Use the dative with confidence: after geben, helfen and gefallen, after aus, bei, mit, nach, seit, von and zu, and in the two-way prepositions where wo takes the dative and wohin the accusative.
Handle separable and reflexive verbs, and place the prefix at the end where it belongs.
Build subordinate clauses with weil, dass, wenn and ob, sending the verb to the end.
Tell a story in the past using the perfect, and use the Präteritum of sein, haben and the modals.
Compare things with the comparative and superlative, and make polite requests with würde and könnte.

descriptors: I can understand phrases and the highest frequency vocabulary related to areas of most immediate personal relevance, for example very basic personal and family information, shopping, local area, employment.
I can catch the main point in short, clear, simple messages and announcements.
I can read very short, simple texts.
I can find specific, predictable information in simple everyday material such as advertisements, prospectuses, menus and timetables, and I can understand short simple personal letters.
I can communicate in simple and routine tasks requiring a simple and direct exchange of information on familiar topics and activities.
I can handle very short social exchanges, even though I can't usually understand enough to keep the conversation going myself.
I can use a series of phrases and sentences to describe in simple terms my family and other people, living conditions, my educational background and my present or most recent job.
I can write short, simple notes and messages relating to matters in areas of immediate need.
I can write a very simple personal letter, for example thanking someone for something.

exam.name: Goethe-Zertifikat A2
exam.description: The second rung of the Goethe ladder: simple communication about familiar everyday matters. Four parts taken as one exam and graded together. This checkpoint practises its format and its points scheme.
exam.passRule: 100 Punkte insgesamt, 25 pro Teil. Bestanden ab 60 von 100 Punkten.

levels.title: Intermediate (Mittelstufe)
levels.milestone: Hold your own on familiar topics, state and justify an opinion, and pass the B1 that citizenship and the settlement permit require.
canDo: Give an opinion and back it with a reason, using deshalb, trotzdem, obwohl and the einerseits, andererseits pair.
Use adjective endings after der, ein and no article at all, which is where most B1 written marks are won or lost.
Handle the passive with werden, relative clauses in every case, and the genitive in writing.
Use the Konjunktiv II for hypotheses, advice and polite requests, and Wechselpräpositionen without thinking about them.
Write a structured letter or forum post of about 80 words, and take part in a paired planning discussion the way the Sprechen module asks.

descriptors: I can understand the main points of clear standard speech on familiar matters regularly encountered in work, school, leisure and so on.
I can understand the main point of many radio or TV programmes on current affairs or topics of personal or professional interest when the delivery is relatively slow and clear.
I can understand texts that consist mainly of high frequency everyday or job-related language.
I can understand the description of events, feelings and wishes in personal letters.
I can deal with most situations likely to arise whilst travelling in an area where the language is spoken.
I can enter unprepared into conversation on topics that are familiar, of personal interest or pertinent to everyday life, for example family, hobbies, work, travel and current events.
I can connect phrases in a simple way in order to describe experiences and events, my dreams, hopes and ambitions.
I can briefly give reasons and explanations for opinions and plans, and narrate the plot of a book or film and describe my reactions.
I can write simple connected text on topics which are familiar or of personal interest.
I can write personal letters describing experiences and impressions.

exam.name: Goethe-Zertifikat B1
exam.description: The certificate German authorities accept for citizenship and the settlement permit. It is modular: each part can be sat separately and only a failed module needs retaking, so each module has to be cleared on its own. This checkpoint practises that format and applies the modular pass rule.
sections: Sprechen, speaking, about 15 minutes, in pairs
exam.passRule: Modulare Prüfung. Bestanden ab 60% in JEDEM Modul, nicht im Durchschnitt.
